sections.title: Overview
sections.content: This guideline covers safe issue of site-based aids and simple rehab equipment such as crutches, frames, canes, braces, and basic support devices.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Need for temporary walking aid or support device
- Need for simple braces or straps to allow safe function
- Concern that a device is being used poorly or is no longer appropriate

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Issuing equipment without fit, instruction, and review can create falls risk
- The right device is only helpful if the patient can use it safely in the actual site environment
- Equipment plans should include return, replacement, and review expectations

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Check whether the patient can safely use the device immediately and whether supervision is needed
- Fit and demonstrate the aid rather than just handing it out
- Escalate if even with the device the patient remains unsafe to mobilise

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Match the aid to the impairment, terrain, and expected duration of use
- Provide clear instruction on stairs, transfers, uneven ground, and what deterioration should prompt re-contact
- Review whether the device can be downgraded or ceased as function improves

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Equipment issue is a clinical intervention, not just a logistics task
